candidate_summary: CHILOTE-PALAFITO: three-tier persistent memory architecture for long-context LLM serving inspired by Chiloé palafito stilt-houses. Tier-0 Pier (HBM-resident KV-cache, K-token eviction), Tier-1 Dwelling (DRAM mid-context, importance-LRU eviction), Tier-2 Attic (persistent parametric, periodic compression). Adds (a) shared flexible-pile base-embedding F_base anchored across all tiers with tidal ΔF distribution-shift updates, and (b) wooden-bridge gated sliding-window cross-tier read/write paths between (Tier-0,Tier-1) and (Tier-1,Tier-2). Claims novelty over Tutti/Strata/MTDS/LMCache via shared-base anchor + gated cross-tier bridges + tidal-flux adaptation.

scoring_methodology.kw: Substring/token presence of any of 8 content_words tokens in title+snippet (case-insensitive). For compound phrases, any salient subtoken (e.g., 'KV-cache', 'HBM-DRAM-SSD', 'cross-tier', 'adaptive eviction', 'multi-tier', 'embedding', 'persistent') counts when clearly aligned with the content_word's semantic head.
scoring_methodology.sem: Independent cosine-like estimate over 0-1, recomputed from semantic alignment of result title+snippet to CHILOTE-PALAFITO 3-tier HBM/DRAM/persistent + shared base embedding + gated cross-tier sliding-window + adaptive eviction mechanism.
scoring_methodology.func: v5 rubric: 0.7 = same artefact (multi-tier LLM KV cache hierarchy) + same effect (long-context memory persistence / cross-tier traffic reduction / adaptive eviction / shared anchor) achieved via different mechanism. 0.9+ = essentially same mechanism.

results.title: TTKV Temporal-Tiered KV Cache Long-Context LLM (2604.19769)
results.matched_effect_cluster: temporal-tiered KV cache + cross-tier traffic reduction
results.rationale: Temporal-tiered KV cache explicitly reduces cross-tier traffic on long context — same artefact (multi-tier KV hierarchy) and same effect cluster (cross-tier traffic minimization) as Pier/Dwelling/Attic with wooden-bridge access. Mechanism differs only in lacking shared base-embedding anchor.

results.title: Tutti SSD-Backed KV Cache Long-Context LLM (2605.03375)
keyword_matches: tiered KV-cache HBM-DRAM-SSD persistence levels (3-tier HBM-DRAM-SSD)
results.rationale: Exact 3-tier HBM-DRAM-SSD KV cache hierarchy with layer-wise pipelining maps directly to Pier/Dwelling/Attic architecture. Candidate itself cites Tutti as the artefact baseline being extended.

results.title: Strata Hierarchical Context Caching Long-Context LLM (2508.18572)
keyword_matches: tiered KV-cache HBM-DRAM-SSD persistence levels (hierarchical/tier-memory partial)
results.matched_effect_cluster: hierarchical context caching with decoupled tier memory layouts
results.rationale: Hierarchical context caching with GPU-assisted I/O and decoupled tier memory layouts is the multi-tier persistent memory hierarchy artefact. Same effect cluster (long-context tiered caching); different mechanism (GPU-IO decoupling vs shared base anchor).

results.title: MTDS Multi-Tier Dynamic Storage KV Cache LLM 2025
keyword_matches: tiered KV-cache HBM-DRAM-SSD persistence levels (Multi-Tier+KV Cache)
tidal-flux adaptive eviction multi-level (adaptive hierarchical eviction)
results.matched_effect_cluster: multi-tier dynamic storage with adaptive eviction
results.rationale: Hierarchical multi-tier storage with selective KV reuse + adaptive hierarchical eviction is the same artefact and same effect cluster as tidal-flux adaptive eviction across tiers. Differs in lacking shared embedding anchor.

results.title: EG-MLA Embedding-Gated Multi-Head Latent Attention (2509.16686)
keyword_matches: flexible-pile foundation shared embedding adapter (embedding gating)
results.rationale: Token-specific embedding gating modulating compressed KV is functionally aligned with the wooden-bridge gated cross-tier read/write and the embedding-anchor concept. Same effect (gated KV access through embedding); different artefact granularity (intra-layer latent vs cross-tier).

results.title: Agent Memory Q4 KV Cache Multi-Agent LLM Edge (2603.04428)
keyword_matches: tiered KV-cache HBM-DRAM-SSD persistence levels (persistent+tier-based KV cache)
results.matched_effect_cluster: persistent quantized KV cache + tier-based hierarchy
results.rationale: Persistent Q4 KV cache below the prompt with tier-based memory hierarchy for multi-agent edge inference covers the Attic (persistent parametric) tier with compression. Same effect cluster (persistent compressed memory tier); different mechanism (quantization vs parametric compression).

results.title: ContextKeeper Head-Specific KV Retention Long-Context LLM
results.rationale: Identifies Context-Anchored (CA) heads with retention policy — functionally the flexible-pile foundation anchor concept at head granularity rather than tier granularity. Same effect cluster (anchored persistence for long-context KV) by different mechanism.

results.title: LMCache Efficient KV Cache Layer Enterprise-Scale LLM
keyword_matches: tiered KV-cache HBM-DRAM-SSD persistence levels (persistent KV-cache)
results.matched_effect_cluster: batched persistent KV anchor for vLLM
results.rationale: Batched KV store/load pipelining with persistent KV-cache anchoring for vLLM is functionally the shared persistent base anchor across serving sessions. Same effect (persistent anchoring + pipelined cross-tier load); different mechanism (batched IO vs shared base embedding).

decision_rationale: All 8 search results clear the semantic threshold (>=0.70) and all 8 clear the functional threshold (>=0.70), with TTKV (R1, func 0.90), Tutti (R2, func 0.88), and Strata (R3, func 0.84) constituting exact-twin prior art for the 3-tier persistent KV cache hierarchy artefact. MTDS (R4) provides the adaptive hierarchical eviction effect that maps onto tidal-flux adaptive eviction. EG-MLA (R5) covers embedding-gated KV access — the wooden-bridge gated mechanism. ContextKeeper (R7) and LMCache (R8) independently cover the 'anchor' concept (head-anchored heads / persistent KV anchor) that the candidate frames as the flexible-pile shared base embedding. The novelty surface (shared base-embedding anchor + gated cross-tier bridges + tidal adaptation) is decomposed across existing artefacts, and the underlying 3-tier KV hierarchy is fully established. Recombination is acknowledged in the candidate's own novelty_claim. Functional saturation (8/8 above 0.7) and multiple distinct effect clusters confirm prior art coverage of every claimed sub-mechanism.
